description: A drop-in, backward-compatible extension of CodeIgniter 3's Query Builder that adds Laravel-inspired features: eager loading, aggregate subqueries, WHERE EXISTS/WHERE HAS conditions, chunking, pagination, and more — while remaining 100% compatible with every existing $this->db->... call. PHP 5.6+ compatible. Identifier quoting is driver-aware (verified on mysqli and sqlite3).

categories.name: Core Query Building
categories.description: select/where/order/limit and related basics, mostly thin CI3-compatible overrides plus a handful of new shorthand helpers.

categories.name: Query Execution & Results
categories.description: Methods that actually run a query (get/get_where/first/value/exists/count_all_results/query) and utility accessors (all_last_query/reset_query/pluck).

categories.name: Eager Loading Relations
categories.description: Load related-table rows onto each result row without N+1 queries (Laravel-style with()).

categories.name: Aggregate Subqueries
categories.description: Add a correlated subquery column (COUNT/SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX/custom expression) per row, and filter on the result with where_aggregate().

categories.name: JOIN-Based Aggregates
categories.description: Lighter alternative to aggregates: one GROUP BY derived-table JOIN instead of one correlated subquery per row.

categories.name: WHERE EXISTS / WHERE HAS
categories.description: Existence and count-threshold conditions against a related table, with call-order-preserving AND/OR glue.

categories.name: Conditional Queries
categories.description: Build dynamic filter chains without a wall of if-statements.

categories.name: Pagination
categories.description: Page-based pagination (paginate()) and the lower-level found-rows counting it's built on (calc_rows()), both using a portable COUNT(*) subquery on every driver.

categories.name: Query Grouping
categories.description: Parenthesized WHERE groups, callback-based or raw start/end pairs, correctly interleaved with every other condition type.

categories.name: Chunking Large Datasets
categories.description: Process large tables in pages without loading everything into memory at once.

categories.name: Transactions
categories.description: Callback-based transaction wrapper with automatic commit/rollback.

categories.name: Advanced: Manual Flush
categories.description: Force-resolve pending (deferred) conditions early, inside a callback context, instead of waiting for get() time. Rarely needed directly — get()/get_compiled_select() call these automatically.

categories.name: The Result Object (CustomQueryBuilderResult)
categories.description: What get()/get_where()/query() return: a CI-result-compatible wrapper with extra methods for relations, found-rows, and pagination.

methods.name: select
methods.description: Thin override of CI3's native select() (kept mainly for IDE return-type chaining). Accepts a comma-separated string or an array of column names/expressions, exactly like stock CodeIgniter.

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false to insert a raw expression (e.g. a subquery or SQL function) without identifier-escaping.

notes: Calling select() again appends fields rather than replacing (native CI3 behavior).

methods.name: add_select
methods.description: Alias for select() — documented separately for readability when you want to signal "add more columns to whatever's already selected", but is functionally identical (CI3's select() already appends).

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false for a raw expression.

examples.description: Add a column after an initial select

methods.name: from
methods.description: Override of CI3's from(). Detects a raw subquery given as a string in the form "(SELECT ...) alias" and injects it directly into the internal FROM list (bypassing CI's identifier escaping, which would otherwise mangle the already-compiled SQL). Also records the table name internally for use by pending where_exists_relation()/relation processors. Array input mixing subqueries and plain table names is handled per-element.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Table name, "table alias", or a raw "(SELECT ...) alias" subquery string (or an array of these).

methods.name: join
methods.description: Thin passthrough override of CI3's native join() (kept for IDE return-type chaining only — no added behavior).

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false for a raw ON condition.

methods.name: limit
methods.description: Thin passthrough override of CI3's native limit() (IDE return-type chaining only).
notes: Usually you pass limit/offset directly to get($table, $limit, $offset) instead of calling this separately.

methods.name: order_by
methods.description: Thin passthrough override of CI3's native order_by() (IDE return-type chaining only).

parameters.name: orderby
parameters.description: Column name, or a raw expression.

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false for a raw expression.

methods.name: group_by
methods.description: Thin passthrough override of CI3's native group_by() (IDE return-type chaining only).

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false for a raw expression.

methods.name: latest
methods.description: Shorthand for order_by($column, 'DESC').

methods.name: oldest
methods.description: Shorthand for order_by($column, 'ASC').

methods.name: where_not
methods.description: Adds a `column != value` condition (value is escaped; column is protected/quoted).

methods.name: or_where_not
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not() — adds `OR column != value`.

methods.name: where_null
methods.description: Adds a `column IS NULL` condition.

methods.name: or_where_null
methods.description: OR-variant of where_null().

methods.name: where_not_null
methods.description: Adds a `column IS NOT NULL` condition.

methods.name: or_where_not_null
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not_null().

methods.name: where_between
methods.description: Adds a `column BETWEEN v1 AND v2` condition. Both bounds are escaped individually. Throws InvalidArgumentException if $values doesn't have exactly 2 elements.
notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if the array doesn't have exactly 2 elements.

methods.name: or_where_between
methods.description: OR-variant of where_between().

methods.name: where_not_between
methods.description: Adds a `column NOT BETWEEN v1 AND v2` condition. Same validation as where_between().

methods.name: or_where_not_between
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not_between().

methods.name: where_in
methods.description: Override of CI3's native where_in(). Numeric arrays are cast via intval() (unquoted); string arrays are escaped individually. Manually replicates CI's AND/OR glue-prefix logic, correctly accounting for this library's deferred-condition machinery. Avoids a PCRE "regular expression is too large" failure CI3's own regex-based validation can throw on very large value lists.

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false to insert values as-is (caller's responsibility to sanitize).

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $key fails column-name validation.
If $values isn't an array or is empty, delegates straight to CI3's native where_in() (same error/behavior as stock CI3).

methods.name: or_where_in
methods.description: OR-variant of where_in().

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false to insert values as-is.

methods.name: where_not_in
methods.description: Adds a `column NOT IN (...)` condition. Same escaping/validation approach as where_in().

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false to insert values as-is.

methods.name: or_where_not_in
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not_in().

parameters.name: escape
parameters.description: Pass false to insert values as-is.

methods.name: order_by_sequence
methods.description: Custom manual ordering: builds a `CASE WHEN col = 'v0' THEN 0 WHEN col = 'v1' THEN 1 ... ELSE N END` expression internally and passes it to order_by(). Rows whose value isn't in $array sort last (ELSE branch).

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column to order by (word chars + one optional dot).

parameters.name: array
parameters.description: Ordered list of values defining the desired sequence.

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $array is empty/not an array or $column fails validation.

methods.name: order_by_relation
methods.description: Order by a column from a related table using a correlated subquery, without a JOIN. Registers a deferred ORDER BY resolved at get()/get_compiled_select() time, spliced back into the ORDER BY clause at its original call-order position (so it interleaves correctly with plain order_by() calls). All identifier arguments are validated.

parameters.name: foreignKey
parameters.description: Column in the related table to match.

parameters.name: localKey
parameters.description: Column in the main table (qualify with a table prefix to avoid ambiguity, e.g. 'quotation.idmarketing').

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column in the related table to order by.

examples.description: Order by a related user's name, without a JOIN

notes: Underlying subquery uses a bare LIMIT 1 (not portable to SQL Server/Oracle syntax — fine on MySQL/SQLite/Postgres).

methods.name: get
methods.description: Executes the query and returns a CustomQueryBuilderResult. Automatically resolves any pending eager-loading relations, aggregate subqueries, WHERE EXISTS/HAS conditions, groups, and calc_rows()/paginate() found-rows counting before compiling. This is the terminal call for almost every query chain in this library.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Table name (optional if from() was already called).

notes: If paginate() was called and $limit is left null here, get() computes LIMIT/OFFSET from the page/per_page you passed to paginate() — an explicit $limit/$offset here always overrides that.

methods.name: get_where
methods.description: Convenience wrapper: applies from(), where($where) (if an array is given), limit() (if given), then delegates to get() if any relation/aggregate/group state is pending, or runs a lightweight direct query otherwise.

parameters.name: where
parameters.description: Associative array of column => value conditions.

methods.name: first
methods.description: limit(1)->get($table), then returns row() (an object) if any row matched, otherwise null.

returns.description: The first matching row, or null.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Pre-execution shortcut: runs select($column)->limit(1)->get($table)->row() and pulls just that one column's value, more efficient than fetching a full row you only need one field from (adds an implicit LIMIT 1). Handles dotted table.column by taking the part after the last dot.

returns.description: The column's value, or null if no row matched.

examples.code:
$email = $this->db->where('id', 1)->value('email', 'users');
// Equivalent to: SELECT `email` FROM users WHERE id = 1 LIMIT 1

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $column fails column-name validation.
There is also a post-execution $result->value($column) on CustomQueryBuilderResult, which pulls from an already-fetched row instead of running a new query.

methods.name: exists
methods.description: limit(1)->get($table), returns whether any row matched.

returns.description: True if at least one row matches.

methods.name: doesnt_exist
methods.description: Inverse of exists() — !exists($table).

methods.name: count_all_results
methods.description: Flushes all pending query state, temporarily strips eager-loading relations (a count doesn't need them), and delegates to CI3's native count_all_results(). Ignores eager-loading by design — counts the base query only; aggregate subqueries (with_count/with_sum/etc.) still apply since those live in SELECT/WHERE, not the separate eager-loading pipeline.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: Whether to reset query state afterward. Pass false to keep the current WHERE conditions for a following get() call on the same builder (e.g. building a paginated total + page from the same filters).

examples.description: Total + page from the same filters

methods.name: get_compiled_select
methods.description: Flushes pending query state (same as count_all_results()), strips eager-loading relations, and delegates to CI3's native get_compiled_select() to return the raw SQL string without executing it. Ignores eager-loading by design (same rationale as count_all_results()) — aggregate subqueries still show up since they live in SELECT/WHERE.

parameters.name: reset
parameters.description: Whether to reset query state afterward.

examples.description: Inspect the SQL a chain would produce

methods.name: query
methods.description: Override so raw SQL keeps working like native CI, while SELECT results are still wrapped in a CustomQueryBuilderResult. If with_one()/with_many() relations are queued beforehand, they're still resolved as separate follow-up queries matched back onto the raw SQL's results in PHP. Does NOT support anything that needs to splice into THIS query's SELECT/WHERE/JOIN before compilation — top-level with_count()/with_sum()/etc., where_has(), where_exists_relation(), where_aggregate(), join_count()/join_sum()/etc. queued beforehand are silently discarded (and cleared, so they don't leak into the next call either).

returns.description: Wrapped result for SELECT; plain bool for write statements (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE), same as native CI3.

examples.code: $ok = $this->db->query("UPDATE users SET status = 'inactive' WHERE id = 1"); // returns bool

methods.name: all_last_query
methods.description: Returns every query executed by the most recent get() call, as an array of SQL strings — the main query plus one per eager-loaded relation, in execution order. Falls back to a single-element array (native last_query()) for plain queries with no eager loading.

methods.name: reset_query
methods.description: Clears every custom queue this library maintains (eager-loading relations, all pending_* condition/aggregate queues, calc_rows/pagination state, group-tracking stacks, temp table name, call-order bookkeeping) in addition to CI3's own native reset. Important when cloning a builder instance to construct a subquery.
notes: Called automatically by get()/get_where() as part of their internal flow — you rarely need to call this yourself, except between independent query chains reusing the same $this->db instance in the same request.

methods.name: pluck
methods.description: Returns a flat array of a single column's values across every matching row. Dot notation can reach into an eager-loaded relation.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column to pluck (supports 'relation.column' dot notation for a loaded relation).

methods.name: with
methods.description: The generic engine behind with_one()/with_many() — both are one-line wrappers around this with $multiple fixed to false/true respectively. Prefer with_one()/with_many() for readability; with() is identical functionality with multiplicity spelled out as a boolean.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: Table name, or a single-element ['table' => 'alias'] array.

parameters.name: foreignKey
parameters.description: Column(s) on the related table (array for composite keys).

parameters.name: localKey
parameters.description: Column(s) on the main table (must match foreignKey's cardinality).

parameters.name: multiple
parameters.description: true = array of matching rows (with_many style), false = single row (with_one style).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void — add WHERE/order_by/nested relations scoped to this relation's subquery.

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if the relation array doesn't have exactly one element, if $multiple isn't a strict bool, or if foreignKey/localKey cardinalities don't match.

methods.name: with_one
methods.description: Eager-load a single related record per row (belongsTo/hasOne-style). When multiple relation rows match the same local key, the FIRST matching row is kept by default (respects an order_by() inside the callback) — see CustomQueryBuilder::FIX_WITH_ONE_ORDER_BY.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void — scope the relation subquery (where/order_by/nested with_*).

methods.name: with_many
methods.description: Eager-load an array of related records per row (hasMany-style).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void — scope the relation subquery.

notes: $relation accepts a plain string or a single-element ['table' => 'alias'] array — any other array shape throws InvalidArgumentException.
foreignKey/localKey accept either a single column string or an equal-length array for composite keys.

methods.name: with_count
methods.description: Adds a correlated COUNT(*) subquery column per row.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

notes: Runs one extra correlated subquery per row in the result set — see JOIN-Based Aggregates (join_count) for a lighter alternative on large result sets.

methods.name: with_sum
methods.description: Adds a correlated SUM(column) subquery column per row. $column can be a plain column name, or (with $is_custom_expression = true) a validated custom SQL expression.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column name, or a custom expression (see $is_custom_expression).

parameters.name: is_custom_expression
parameters.description: Pass true to allow a whitelisted mathematical expression instead of a plain column name — see Security.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

notes: See with_calculation for arbitrary aggregate expressions with a dedicated alias-first signature.

methods.name: with_avg
methods.description: Same shape as with_sum(), using AVG(column) instead of SUM(column).

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column name, or a custom expression.

parameters.name: is_custom_expression
parameters.description: Pass true for a whitelisted custom expression.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

methods.name: with_max
methods.description: Same shape as with_sum(), using MAX(column).

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column name, or a custom expression.

parameters.name: is_custom_expression
parameters.description: Pass true for a whitelisted custom expression.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

methods.name: with_min
methods.description: Same shape as with_sum(), using MIN(column).

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column name, or a custom expression.

parameters.name: is_custom_expression
parameters.description: Pass true for a whitelisted custom expression.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

methods.name: with_calculation
methods.description: Add an arbitrary aggregate expression (not limited to a single SUM/AVG/etc.) as a correlated subquery column. $expression goes through the same custom-expression security validation as with_sum(..., true).

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: ['table' => 'alias'] — alias is required here (used as the result column name).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra WHERE conditions inside the subquery.

notes: Allowed in expressions: + - * / %, SUM AVG COUNT MIN MAX, DATEDIFF TIMESTAMPDIFF (MySQL-only — not yet translated for other drivers), CASE WHEN...THEN...END, ROUND FLOOR CEIL ABS, and a handful of other whitelisted functions — see Security. Dangerous keywords/expressions are rejected before ever reaching the database.

methods.name: where_aggregate
methods.description: Filter by a previously-registered with_sum()/with_avg()/with_calculation()/etc. alias. Wraps the underlying subquery in COALESCE(subquery, 0) so a row with zero related rows (NULL aggregate) still evaluates correctly instead of silently being excluded. Preserves the exact call-order position relative to other where()/or_where()/group() calls in the same chain.

parameters.name: condition
parameters.description: "alias operator" e.g. "total_spent >=", or "alias BETWEEN"/"alias NOT BETWEEN".

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Comparison value, or a 2-element [min, max] array for BETWEEN.

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if the alias was never registered via a with_*() call, or if a BETWEEN value array doesn't have exactly 2 elements.

methods.name: or_where_aggregate
methods.description: OR-variant of where_aggregate().

methods.name: join_count
methods.description: Lighter alternative to with_count(): scans the relation table ONCE via a GROUP BY derived table, then LEFT JOINs it in — instead of one correlated subquery per row. Prefer this over with_count() on large result sets.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra conditions inside the derived-table subquery.

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $relation is empty/whitespace-only.

methods.name: join_sum
methods.description: join_count()'s SUM(column) counterpart — same derived-table-JOIN mechanism.
notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $column is missing or fails column-name validation.

methods.name: join_avg
methods.description: join_count()'s AVG(column) counterpart.

methods.name: join_min
methods.description: join_count()'s MIN(column) counterpart.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column to find the minimum of.

methods.name: join_max
methods.description: join_count()'s MAX(column) counterpart.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Column to find the maximum of.

methods.name: join_calculation
methods.description: join_count()'s arbitrary-expression counterpart — same validated-expression rules as with_calculation(), but via a derived-table JOIN instead of a correlated subquery.

parameters.name: relation
parameters.description: ['table' => 'alias'] — alias used as the result column name.

methods.name: where_exists
methods.description: Raw EXISTS(...) subquery with full manual control — you build the entire subquery yourself in the callback.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void — build the correlated subquery (select/from/where).

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $callback isn't callable.
For the common "just check a related table has any/N matching rows" case, where_exists_relation()/where_has() are simpler — this raw form is for when you need arbitrary subquery logic.

methods.name: where_not_exists
methods.description: NOT EXISTS(...) counterpart to where_exists().

methods.name: or_where_exists
methods.description: OR-variant of where_exists().

methods.name: or_where_not_exists
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not_exists().

methods.name: where_exists_relation
methods.description: Simplified WHERE EXISTS that auto-joins on the given keys instead of writing the subquery by hand. Works equally well whether from('table') was called up front or get('table') at the end — the parent table is resolved lazily either way. Supports composite keys via arrays.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra conditions inside the EXISTS subquery.

parameters.name: disable_pending_process
parameters.description: Advanced/rarely needed: skip queuing through the ordering-preserving pending mechanism. Exists for edge cases needing the same reordering used inside group() even outside one.

examples.code:
$this->db->from('users')->where_exists_relation('orders', 'user_id', 'id');
// WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders WHERE orders.user_id = users.id)

methods.name: where_not_exists_relation
methods.description: NOT EXISTS counterpart to where_exists_relation(). Also aliased as where_doesnt_have().

methods.name: or_where_exists_relation
methods.description: OR-variant of where_exists_relation().

methods.name: or_where_not_exists_relation
methods.description: OR-variant of where_not_exists_relation().

methods.name: where_has
methods.description: Existence check with a count threshold. With the default count (>=1), compiles to the same plain EXISTS shape as where_exists_relation() (no COUNT(*) overhead). A 4th-argument shorthand lets you pass just the operator when you don't need a callback.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Extra conditions inside the subquery, OR (shorthand) the operator string when you don't need a callback.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Threshold to compare the related row count against.

methods.name: or_where_has
methods.description: OR-variant of where_has(). Same parameter shape.

methods.name: where_doesnt_have
methods.description: Documented alias of where_not_exists_relation() — same NOT EXISTS subquery shape, more Laravel-familiar name.

methods.name: or_where_doesnt_have
methods.description: OR-variant / alias of or_where_not_exists_relation().

methods.name: when
methods.description: Run a callback only if $condition is truthy, with an optional else-callback. Great for building dynamic filter chains without a wall of if-statements.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void, run when $condition is truthy.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void, run when $condition is falsy.

methods.name: unless
methods.description: Inverse of when() — runs the callback when $condition is falsy.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void, run when $condition is falsy.

parameters.name: default
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder): void, run when $condition is truthy.

methods.name: search
methods.description: Multi-column LIKE search shorthand, wrapped in its own group().

parameters.name: or
parameters.description: true = OR the column conditions together; false = AND them.

notes: Blank/empty entries in $columns are skipped without producing invalid SQL.
An empty $columns array leaves the query unchanged (no-op, not an error).

methods.name: paginate
methods.description: Laravel-style page-based pagination, built on calc_rows(). Computes LIMIT/OFFSET from $per_page/$page for you. Argument order deliberately matches Laravel's own paginate($perPage, ...) — a single-argument ->paginate(20) means "20 per page", not "page 20". Uses a portable COUNT(*) subquery on every driver (including MySQL — SQL_CALC_FOUND_ROWS is not used anywhere in this library).

examples.code:
$result = $this->db->where('status', 'active')
    ->paginate(20, 2)   // 20 per page, page 2
    ->get('users');

$result->result();          // 20 rows for page 2
$result->found_rows();      // total rows across every page, e.g. 347
$result->current_page();    // 2
$result->per_page();        // 20
$result->last_page();       // 18
$result->has_more_pages();  // true
$result->from();            // 21
$result->to();               // 40

examples.description: Full JSON-shaped response in one call

notes: An explicit $limit/$offset passed to get() overrides paginate()'s computed values.
Throws InvalidArgumentException if $per_page or $page is not an integer >= 1.
There's no next_page_url/prev_page_url — this library sits below the HTTP/routing layer and has no request context to build URLs from. Build links from current_page()/last_page() in your controller.

methods.name: calc_rows
methods.description: Lower-level building block behind paginate(): marks the query so the next get() also computes the total row count the query would have matched without LIMIT, via a portable COUNT(*) subquery (used on every driver, including MySQL — never SQL_CALC_FOUND_ROWS, which is deprecated since MySQL 8.0.17 and never actually cheaper). Reach for this directly when you want the found-rows total but not page-number-based pagination (e.g. you already compute your own offset).
notes: Runs two queries under the hood — the page of data, and a SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (<your query, no LIMIT>) — same total work as calling count_all_results() and get() separately, just as one method chain.

methods.name: get_found_rows
methods.description: Old-style (pre-$result->found_rows()) access to the calc_rows()/paginate() total: reads it straight off $this->db right after the query, instead of off the returned result object. Kept for backward compatibility.

returns.description: The total from the most recent calc_rows()/paginate() query, or 0 if none was made (or if called without calc_rows()/paginate() first).

notes: Prefer $result->found_rows() in new code — it's tied to the specific result object rather than to whatever $this->db was doing most recently, so it can't accidentally read a stale/wrong value if you build another query on the same builder afterward.

methods.name: group
methods.description: Wraps conditions built inside the callback in a parenthesized AND-joined group. Preserves position relative to where_has()/where_exists_relation()/where_aggregate()/plain where() calls before or after it, however they're all interleaved.

methods.name: or_group
methods.description: OR-variant of group().

methods.name: group_start
methods.description: Raw opening half of a group — reach for this (paired with group_end()) instead of group()/or_group() when you need to open and close the bracket at two different points in your code (e.g. across an if), rather than inside a single callback. If nothing ends up added between group_start() and group_end(), the empty bracket is dropped automatically instead of emitting invalid "( )" SQL.
notes: Nests freely with itself, with group()/or_group(), and with where_has()/where_exists_relation()/where_aggregate() in any combination.

methods.name: group_end
methods.description: Closing half of the raw group_start()/group_end() pair.

examples.description: See group_start() for a full example.

methods.name: chunk
methods.description: Offset-based chunking: processes a large table in pages of $size rows without loading everything into memory. The callback receives an array of objects (not arrays) plus the current page number, and can return false to stop early.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(object[] $rows, int $page): bool|void — return false to stop.

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Table name (optional if from() was already called).

methods.name: chunk_by_id
methods.description: ID-based chunking (no gaps/duplicates if rows are inserted/deleted mid-run, unlike offset-based chunk()). Throws InvalidArgumentException if $column isn't present in a fetched row (e.g. you selected specific columns and forgot to include the ordering column).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(object[] $rows): bool|void — return false to stop.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Ordering/cursor column (must be present in the selected columns).

methods.name: transaction
methods.description: Wraps a callback in a database transaction with automatic commit/rollback. On any thrown exception inside the callback, the transaction is rolled back; by default the exception is swallowed and the method returns false — pass $strict = true to have it re-thrown instead (still after rolling back).

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: callable(CustomQueryBuilder $db): mixed — receives the builder instance as its argument.

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description: If true, re-throws the original exception after rollback instead of returning false.

returns.description: The callback's return value on success, or false on failure (unless $strict, which re-throws).

notes: Throws InvalidArgumentException if $callback isn't callable.

methods.name: process_where_has
methods.description: Manually force-resolves any queued where_has()/where_doesnt_have() conditions into the query immediately, instead of waiting for get() time. For use inside callback contexts where you need the condition applied before further chaining/inspection within that same callback.
notes: get() calls this automatically as part of its normal flow — most code never needs to call it directly.

methods.name: process_where_exists
methods.description: Manually force-resolves any queued where_exists_relation() conditions into actual EXISTS/NOT EXISTS subqueries immediately. Requires the parent table name explicitly (unlike process_where_has()) so bare local keys can be qualified correctly.

parameters.name: parent_table
parameters.description: The main table name to qualify bare local keys against.

notes: get() calls this automatically as part of its normal flow.

methods.name: process_aggregates
methods.description: Manually force-resolves queued with_count()/with_sum()/etc. SELECT-column aggregates immediately instead of at get() time.
notes: get() calls this automatically.

methods.name: process_where_aggregates
methods.description: Manually force-resolves queued where_aggregate()/or_where_aggregate() conditions immediately.

parameters.name: context_table
parameters.description: Override which table qualifies bare local keys, instead of relying on the current FROM table.

notes: get() calls this automatically.

methods.name: process_join_aggregates
methods.description: Manually force-resolves queued join_count()/join_sum()/etc. derived-table JOINs immediately.
notes: get() calls this automatically.

methods.name: result
methods.description: Returns every row as an array of objects (same shape as native CI). Memoized — computed once, reused on repeat calls.

methods.name: result_array
methods.description: Returns every row as an array of associative arrays. Memoized.

methods.name: row
methods.description: Returns a single row (by index, default first) as an object, or null if that index doesn't exist. Reuses result()'s cache if already computed.

methods.name: row_array
methods.description: Array-form counterpart to row().

methods.name: num_rows
methods.description: Row count for this result (computed at construction time).

methods.name: found_rows
methods.description: Total row count without LIMIT, from a preceding calc_rows()/paginate() query.

returns.description: Null if calc_rows()/paginate() wasn't used for this query.

methods.name: current_page
methods.description: The page number passed to paginate().

methods.name: per_page
methods.description: The per-page size passed to paginate().

methods.name: last_page
methods.description: Total number of pages, computed as max(1, ceil(found_rows / per_page)).

methods.name: has_more_pages
methods.description: Whether there's at least one page after the current one (current_page < last_page).

methods.name: from
methods.description: 1-indexed position of this page's first row within the full (unpaginated) result set. E.g. page 3 at 20/page -> 41.

returns.description: Null if paginate() wasn't used, or the page/result is empty.

methods.name: to
methods.description: 1-indexed position of this page's last row within the full result set, capped at found_rows() (a partial last page doesn't overshoot the true total).

returns.description: Null if paginate() wasn't used, or the page/result is empty.

methods.name: to_pagination_array
methods.description: Packages result_array() plus every pagination field into one Laravel-shaped array, ready for e.g. a JSON API response.

returns.description: {data, current_page, per_page, total, last_page, from, to, has_more_pages}

methods.name: key_by
methods.description: Re-indexes the result set by a column value (Laravel Collection::keyBy()-style). $key may be a column name or a callable(row): mixed. A plain string is always treated as a column name even if it happens to collide with a PHP builtin function name.

parameters.name: as_array
parameters.description: Return rows as arrays instead of objects.

returns.description: Keyed by the resolved key value.

notes: If a key repeats, the last matching row wins.
Rows missing the key (or where its value is null) are kept under their original numeric index instead of collapsing into a shared null bucket, and trigger an E_USER_WARNING.

methods.name: value
methods.description: Post-execution: pulls a single column's value off the first row of an already-fetched result. Handles dotted 'relation.column' by taking the part after the last dot.

returns.description: Null if there are no rows.

notes: There is also a pre-execution $this->db->value($column, $table) directly on the query builder, which is more efficient since it adds an implicit SELECT + LIMIT 1 instead of fetching a full row you already have.
